rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+)
50% magic rule (M3-M8)
Trust the agentic design - no human backstops
Quality ladder
One exercise = one bounded activity
Modules composable across variants
Three-level completeness
Pattern-recognition LOs need n>=2 instances with contrast
Progression-with-variations is the spine (9b)
Student agency - adults decide
Delegation - student brings, Claude screens
Split mega-exercise when phases = distinct skills + distinct artifacts
Artifact-realism check
Teach principle + technique - default
Auto-fire sim+eval after significant rewrite
Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go; live in prompts not body
rules_evaluated.evidence: L34: "Dig into code quality and structure. Ask Claude: *did you make it better? Why yes. Why no.* The goal is to make a neat and clean fix. Might need a number of nudges. You steer." — this is a mandatory teaching move (the code-quality interrogation, a forcing function) left in body prose, not in a fence. Diagnostic per the rule: read each phase prompt-only; the fenced prompts (1/2/3) cover tests-first and root-cause depth but the quality-cleanup move lands only in body, so a speed-runner skipping body misses it.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either move the code-quality 'did you make it better?' move into a fenced (optional) prompt, or confirm it is an opt-in refinement and reword so it reads as optional rather than a mandated step.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Corporate infra = nice-to-have, never forcing function
Personal-first, team promotion via human conversation
Measure silent failure, not just friction
Don't pre-set categories before experiment runs
Verification layers sample DIFFERENT error distributions
Cheap-before-expensive verification ordering (21b)
Rule in memory without forcing function = worse than no rule
Short skeptical pushback on done = re-verify trigger
Cohort execution is trainer-paced + concurrent, not sequential
Host-skill optional; maintainer blocks trainer-only
Reflection beats: in-chat over file-write
Classroom = default delivery; body teaches work, trainer manages room
rules_evaluated.evidence: L42: "*For those finishing early: chatter about making sure the LLM doesn't fake tests or write tests that just pass. How do you catch that efficiently?*" — "chatter" authors a room/cohort-management conversation in the body. The rule bans group/pair/room-share beats in body; trainer cohort-management belongs outside the text or in the maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Move the 'finishing early' chatter prompt into the maintainer block (decision point / fast-finisher recovery), or reframe it as a solo work prompt rather than a discuss-with-the-room cue.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Eval = fixed yardstick; thing measured learns
Reading burden for verification = banned. Claude audits Claude
Self-report + self-audit charity - verify at artifact
Offered structures need forced-engagement mechanic
Source-type blindness on ingestion
Task selection drives outcome - name floor AND ceiling
Maintainer-block hedge naming section's own failure mode = cut-signal (34b)
Audience tier determines authoring substrate
Verification machinery needs smoke-test before trust
Context-loading tiers as staged progression
Removals are strategic - ask before cutting
Symmetric visible-mechanics > runtime fork
Load-bearing platform feature = no fallback. Gate or redesign
Eval architecture - partition by evidence source
Plug points + UX
Per-exercise leap test - 3 observable Monday outcomes
Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch
Heavy fan-out prompts get body recovery callout
Save belongs with authoring, not loading
Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias -> body acknowledgment callout
Deliberate-antipattern prompts = class-judge exempt, flagged-by-design
Session widget - shape; place by learning flow
Widgets carry semantics, not just emphasis (52b)
Pre-action framing doesn't preview prompt's payoff
Engineer-audience audit fixes default to body callout, not forcing function
Multi-session ships as packaging, not new primitive
/clear = tool, not ceremony
Inbound links preserve pedagogical beat across reference restructuring
Scan at the unit the rule names, not convenient larger one
Verify mechanism before proposing surgery
Acting on a flagged item - verify before fixing (61a)
Callouts next to prompts: steer, don't pre-empt
rules_evaluated.evidence: L15: "If Claude starts spinning up plenty of tests, or the change creeps past the bug, that's sprawl. Steer it back to one test that proves the bug and the smallest fix that passes it." — this callout names two specific failure modes (test-suite sprawl, scope creep) rather than the generalising steering meta-move (notice -> ask Claude why -> relax/narrow the requirement). Per the rule's rot test, if the prompt mechanics change the named failure mode can disappear while the callout keeps pointing at a ghost.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Consider reshaping the 'Small fix, small test' callout toward the generalising steer (notice the over-run, ask Claude to justify, narrow it) so it survives prompt-mechanic changes; or accept the named-failure-mode form if it is judged load-bearing for this trivial-bug beat.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Persistent TODO surfaces stale by default - audit before acting
Maintainer blocks point, don't restate
